name: FareHarbor Tour Booking API
what_it_does: FareHarbor REST API for tour and activity booking platform. Enables AI agents to manage availability and bookings, retrieve item and package listings, create reservations, handle customer data, and integrate activity booking data into travel and hospitality workflows. FareHarbor is a leading reservation platform for tours, activities, and experiences used by operators worldwide.
use_cases: Querying tour availability and pricing from travel booking agents
Creating reservations from customer service automation agents
Syncing booking data with CRM from operations integration agents
Managing capacity and availability for experience operators from inventory agents
not_for: Hotel room reservations without activity/tour focus
Restaurant dining reservations without activity experiences
best_when: A tour operator, activity company, or travel aggregator using FareHarbor wants AI agents to check availability, create bookings, and manage reservation data programmatically.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Customer booking and payment data is personal. Booking modifications may be subject to cancellation policies and operator agreements.

auth.notes: FareHarbor uses API key authentication in the request headers. Separate keys for sandbox (Waikiki company) and production environments. Keys are company-scoped per operator.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No platform fee for operators; FareHarbor earns via booking fee on transactions
pricing.paid_tiers: Transaction fee model; API access available for operators and distribution partners
pricing.notes: Booking.com-owned since 2018. Free to operators (transaction fee model). API access for operators and OTA/distribution partners. Sandbox company (Waikiki) for testing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in API reference

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Customer booking and payment data; PCI-DSS compliance for payment handling
No public MCP server — REST API with API key auth
Booking.com-owned — rate and cancellation policies may reflect Booking.com distribution norms
Sandbox environment uses 'Waikiki' test company — use this for all development
Booking creation requires correct capacity/availability checks to avoid overbooking
agent_readiness.security_notes: Customer booking and payment data. PCI-DSS, SOC2. API key auth. US-only. Booking.com-owned. No granular scopes.

security_score_components.security_notes: Customer booking and payment data. PCI-DSS, SOC2. API key. US-only. No granular scopes.
